requirement: Buyer can initiate the supplier onboarding process by providing basic supplier information.
Supplier registration process should involve internal approval workflow.
Supplier should receive an invitation to register with AECI and complete due diligence steps after internal approval.
Supplier information should be accurate and current.
Supplier certifications and contact information should be visible.
Multiple channels for managing supplier information should be eliminated.
Supplier status should be managed, including purchase block, payment block, supplier deactivation, and reactivation.
Approval workflows should involve Procurement, Finance, General Accounting, and Master Data teams.
Coupa SIM should integrate with SAP for supplier creation, updates, and activation.
Internal users can register new suppliers in Coupa for urgent or business-critical situations, adhering to governance and approval controls.
Internal users can update existing supplier information in Coupa, adhering to governance and approval controls.
Internal users can change the status of a supplier in Coupa, including blocking purchase orders or payments, deactivating, or reactivating the supplier.
Internal users can create a one-time vendor in Coupa with a simplified approval flow and reduced data requirements.
Users can view the status of each supplier in the Suppliers table, including Coupa Supplier Portal (CSP) statuses.
The system must accommodate geography-specific requirements, including field-level requirements and attachments, based on local guidelines and regulations.
The system must support auto-translation of standard information on the user interface for multiple languages and accommodate custom translations for geography-specific requirements.
